senses.definition: a combination of skills or techniques that can help in achieving one's goals

senses.definition: a variety of plans, ideas, etc. that one can use if need arises

examples.x: Looking for another string to his bow, he turned to art and design, for which he had always shown a talent.
Learning a new language is just one more string to her bow, making her a more valuable asset to the company.

senses.definition: a strategy in which one deliberately refrains from taking any action in order to gain advantage later on

examples.x: Although most people were relieved that the waiting game was over, the first days and nights were nerve-racking.
Lucy only wished that she could have more of the patience required to play a waiting game.
Job seekers often play the waiting game after submitting applications, hoping for interview invitations.
During a medical investigation, patients often find themselves in a waiting game as they await test results and a diagnosis.
Investors sometimes have to play the waiting game as they wait for the right market conditions to make profitable trades.

senses.definition: to take a moment to ponder what one is about to say

examples.x: He spoke very slowly, weighing his words.
Before addressing the sensitive topic, she paused to weigh her words, ensuring she didn't offend anyone.
In a job interview, it's crucial to weigh your words to present yourself in the best light.
When discussing a controversial subject with friends, it's wise to weigh your words to avoid unnecessary arguments.

senses.definition: to achieve two goals at the same time only by taking one action

examples.x: I killed two birds with one stone and picked the kids up on the way to the supermarket.
We can kill two birds with one stone by dropping off the mail when we go to the grocery store.
By working from home, she can kill two birds with one stone ‚Äď save time on commuting and spend more time with her family.
Buying a hybrid car not only reduces emissions but also saves money on fuel, killing two birds with one stone.
Planning a picnic at the park allowed them to enjoy the outdoors and celebrate their anniversary, killing two birds with one stone.

senses.definition: to refrain from making a decision or taking any action until one acquires more data on the situation or how it develops
a very friendly and gentle manner that is used to hide one's firm determination or ruthless mindset

examples.x: He may appear to rule with a velvet glove, but he is really quite cruel.
It is no longer exercising power through velvet gloves.
She managed to resolve the conflict with a velvet glove by calmly addressing the issue while asserting her authority.
The CEO's velvet glove approach in negotiations allowed her to maintain a pleasant demeanor while securing a favorable deal for the company.
The politician's speech was delivered with a velvet glove, using polite language to criticize his opponents' policies.

senses.definition: the influence children have on their parents or caregivers to buy or do something through continuous requests, demands, or persuasion

examples.x: The kids used their pester power to convince their parents to take them to the amusement park.
Marketers often create advertisements that appeal directly to children, knowing that pester power can be a powerful force in driving sales.
The toy industry relies heavily on pester power, as children's requests for specific toys can heavily influence parents' buying decisions.
Fast-food chains often use promotional tactics aimed at children, capitalizing on pester power to attract families to their restaurants.
Parents often find themselves giving in to pester power during trips to the grocery store, as children request sugary snacks or cereals they've seen advertised.

senses.definition: to approach situations calmly and diplomatically while also having the strength and power to take action if necessary

examples.x: I speak softly and carry a big stick to maintain a peaceful atmosphere.
She speaks softly and carries a big stick to assert her authority when necessary.
The diplomat emphasized the need to speak softly but carry a big stick to achieve peaceful resolutions in international conflicts.
The CEO's management style is to speak softly but carry a big stick, fostering a cooperative work environment while ensuring effective decision-making and accountability.
In negotiations, it's important to speak softly and carry a big stick, conveying a desire for compromise while maintaining leverage.

word_list.word: the oldest trick in the book

senses.definition: an overused method that may still succeed

examples.x: He literally used the oldest trick in the book and still won.
It was the oldest trick in the book - one man distracted me while another stole my wallet.
When he attempted to convince his parents that he was sick to avoid going to school, they saw through it, saying it was the oldest trick in the book.
In detective novels, the criminal often uses the oldest trick in the book‚ÄĒleaving a false trail to divert attention from the real crime.
She didn't fall for his flattery because it was the oldest trick in the book to get what he wanted.
